They do `re.search(...)` on file contents and assert string patterns — none execute the code or test actual behavior. The pass-to-pass tests (cargo check/test/clippy) do execute code but ar

test_not_tautological.reason: While the f2p tests are text-based, they cannot be satisfied by stubs — they require specific constant definitions with specific values (e.g., MAX_INLINE_VALUE_SIZE = 8), specific method bodies containing particular strings (extend_from_slice in put_inline), and specific enum variants. A no-op or empty implementation wouldn't match these patterns. Additionally, cargo check/test must pass, preventing syntactically broken stubs.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is available at /workspace/next.js in the container (via solve.sh applying a git patch). The agent could read solution/solve.sh if accessible, or simply inspect the gold patch. Additionally, since tests are pure text-matching, the agent could grep test files to discover exact patterns needed without understanding the problem.
